 {"id":"bd-h6nz.4.5","title":"Add fuzz coverage/reporting dashboard for target health and trend visibility","description":"## Objective
Track fuzzing effectiveness over time so campaign effort can be prioritized by evidence.

## Scope
1. Per-target metrics: execs/sec, unique crashes, corpus growth, coverage proxy.
2. Historical trend snapshots linked to commit hashes.
3. Alerting thresholds for regressions in fuzz throughput or coverage signals.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reporting artifact is generated automatically after campaign runs.
- Operators can identify underperforming targets quickly.
- Trend history survives across runs and is easy to query.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:51:29.172894341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:14.106529863Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_fuzzing","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:51:29.172894341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:51:29.172894341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":333,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.4.6","title":"Verification Gate: fuzzing/adversarial program (unit + e2e + logging audit)","description":"## Objective

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.5","title":"EPIC: Benchmark Fidelity and Regression Guard Strengthening","description":"# EPIC: Benchmark Fidelity and Regression Guard Strengthening

## Problem Statement
Benchmark scaffolding and baselines exist, but post-parity evolution needs stronger anti-flake methodology, host-profile awareness, and explicit treatment of degraded/runtime-mode performance envelopes.

## Why this matters
Without disciplined benchmark governance, teams either miss regressions or chase noise. We need actionable signals that survive machine variance and evolving workloads.

## Scope
1. Expand benchmark taxonomy beyond current hot paths.
2. Add robust comparison methodology (effect sizes, noise thresholds, guardrails).
3. Validate performance under pressure/degradation modes.
4. Document operator workflow for interpreting and acting on regressions.

## Done Criteria
- Bench suite covers critical latency/throughput/write-amplification surfaces.
- Regression guard emits high-signal failures with clear next steps.
- Performance gates are reproducible across supported host profiles.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:10.465965410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:03:16.623523857Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_benchmarking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:10.465965410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":299,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note:
Benchmarks must optimize decision quality, not vanity numbers. Build guardrails that catch meaningful regressions while suppressing machine-noise churn.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:55:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.5.3","title":"Implement statistically robust regression comparator with anti-flake hysteresis","description":"## Objective
Replace simplistic threshold-only comparisons with a regression gate that accounts for noise, effect size, and repeated confirmation before failing builds.

## Scope
1. Comparator model using confidence/effect-size thresholds.
2. Hysteresis or multi-run confirmation for borderline regressions.
3. Human-readable failure output with likely root-cause dimensions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comparator catches synthetic regressions while suppressing expected noise.
- CI output includes workload, metric, baseline, delta, and confidence context.
- False-positive rate is measurably reduced versus prior guard.

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.6","title":"EPIC: Formal Open-Question Closure and Decision Capture","description":"# EPIC: Formal Open-Question Closure and Decision Capture

## Problem Statement
The comprehensive spec tracks open questions (OQ1..OQ7). Leaving these unresolved creates drift risk: implementation may proceed with implicit assumptions that are not documented, not testable, and not auditable.

## Why this matters
FrankenFS is spec-first. Unresolved questions in critical areas (native-mode boundaries, conflict policy, symbol invalidation, persistence format, etc.) must become explicit decisions with evidence, loss tradeoffs, and integration plans.

## Scope
1. Close each OQ with a decision record (what, why, alternatives rejected).
2. Attach concrete verification and migration implications.
3. Convert accepted decisions into executable tasks and dependencies.
4. Keep parity/spec docs synchronized with implementation reality.

## Done Criteria
- OQ1..OQ7 each have an explicit accepted state (or explicit defer rationale).
- Decision artifacts link to tests/benchmarks where applicable.
- No architecture-critical behavior remains "implied but undocumented".","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:17.241917024Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:00:03.891145751Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_oq_closure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:17.241917024Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":300,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note:
Every OQ closure must include: decision, rejected alternatives, evidence basis, and what tests/docs change because of that decision. "We think" is insufficient.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:55:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.6.5","title":"OQ5 closure: Multi-host repair scope and safety model","description":"## Objective
Close OQ5 by deciding whether multi-host repair coordination is supported, explicitly out of scope, or conditionally allowed with constraints.

## Key Questions
1. Is concurrent multi-host repair permissible in V1.x?
2. What coordination/locking semantics are required to avoid split-brain repair writes?
3. What explicit operator warnings/fail-fast behaviors are needed?

## Deliverables
- Scope decision with rationale and threat model.
- If supported: minimal coordination protocol + failure handling.
- If not supported: explicit detection and blocking behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Multi-host posture is explicit in docs and runtime behavior.
- Unsafe multi-host scenarios cannot proceed silently.
- Evidence/logging clearly report multi-host safety decisions.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9","title":"EPIC: Cross-Epic Verification Orchestration and Log Contract","description":"# EPIC: Cross-Epic Verification Orchestration and Log Contract

## Problem Statement
Current verification expectations are strong but can drift across epics without a shared scenario catalog, log-field contract, and artifact format.

## Why This Matters For Users
- Incident diagnosis is faster when evidence and logs are consistent across subsystems.
- Release confidence improves when E2E scripts and outputs are comparable across tracks.
- Automation is safer with stable machine-readable pass/fail and artifact schemas.

## Scope
1. Shared deterministic E2E scenario catalog and ID conventions.
2. Shared structured logging field contract for verification scenarios.
3. Shared artifact manifest + retention/redaction policy.
4. Shared verification runner conventions and quality checks.

## Done Criteria
- Verification gates across all epics consume the shared contract.
- Drift detection exists for scenario IDs/log fields/artifact schema.
- Operator and CI workflows have one canonical verification contract.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.535727968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:10:10.110144393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.535727968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
